description: Nine-residue HA epitope tag (YPYDVPDYA) derived from influenza A hemagglutinin, used for immunodetection and affinity purification via anti-HA antibodies.

references.authors: Wilson IA, Niman HL, Houghten RA, Cherenson AR, Connolly ML, Lerner RA
references.title: The structure of an antigenic determinant in a protein.

references.authors: Field J, Nikawa J, Broek D, MacDonald B, Rodgers L, Wilson IA, Lerner RA, Wigler M
references.title: Purification of a RAS-responsive adenylyl cyclase complex from Saccharomyces cerevisiae by use of an epitope addition method.

references.authors: Schembri L, Dalibart R, Tomasello F, Legembre P, Ichas F, De Giorgi F
references.title: The HA tag is cleaved and loses immunoreactivity during apoptosis.

label: HA
note: Nine-residue immunodominant epitope tag YPYDVPDYA derived from influenza A hemagglutinin (H3N2 strain A/Aichi/2/1968, UniProt P03437 residues 114-122). Widely used for immunoprecipitation, western blotting, and affinity purification via anti-HA antibodies (e.g., 12CA5, 3F10). Note: the DVPD motif is cleaved by caspases 3 and 7 during apoptosis, causing loss of immunoreactivity.

provenance.sequence_source: UniProt P03437 (HEMA_I68A0, Hemagglutinin, Influenza A virus strain A/Aichi/2/1968 H3N2; Swiss-Prot reviewed canonical), residues 114-122, 100% identity over 9 aa

functional_claims.label: Nine-residue sequence corresponds to an immunodominant antigenic determinant of influenza A hemagglutinin
